what_it_does: This file is a ledger allocation batch manifest: a JSON array listing four substrate records for the claude-ds project, each with a relative path and an absolute root directory.
observations: Contains exactly four records, all with project value claude-ds and root /home/tim/company/design/claude-ds
Includes one lint/config file (_adherence.oxlintrc.json), two archived CSS files under _archive-v1, and one JavaScript bundle (_ds_bundle.js)
Every path begins with an underscore or an archive directory
No symbols are present; symbols[] is empty
Each record repeats the same project and root fields
standouts: The batch mixes current tooling/config, archived design CSS, and a current bundle in one allocation list
The _archive-v1 directory is explicitly named, signaling preserved older versions
The _adherence.oxlintrc.json file suggests a compliance or style-adherence configuration substrate
conventions: Uses a flat JSON array of uniform objects with project, path, and root keys
Absolute filesystem root is provided separately from relative project paths
Underscore prefix denotes tooling or internal files
Archive directory name encodes a version suffix
notes: A small, project-scoped allocation batch that points to a mix of config, archived styles, and a bundle for the claude-ds design system.
questions: What criteria determined which files were included in this batch?
Does wave-001/alloc imply a processing stage, a time window, or a dependency wave?
Are the _archive-v1 files intended to be interpreted as historical references or active inputs?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — the file contains no stated purpose; it actually functions as a manifest of selected substrate paths.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a bounded, project-local set of code/design substrates so an interpretive or build-prep pipeline can locate and process them together.
novelty: The batch deliberately includes archived predecessor CSS alongside current bundle and config, treating historical substrates as part of the interpretable ledger.
contribution: Provides a root-anchored, project-scoped enumeration of substrate paths that a fused system can use as a reliable batch pointer for locating files during later interpretation or build preparation.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch manifest listing four claude-ds project substrates—an adherence config, two archived CSS files, and a design-system bundle—each with project, relative path, and absolute root.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to catalog a specific slice of claude-ds design/code substrates for batch-level ledger interpretation or build-prep processing.
